title: draba rupestris var. legitima
intro: Draba rupestris var. legitima, also known as alpine whitlowgrass or rock whitlowgrass, is a rare, perennial plant species belonging to the mustard family (Brassicaceae). Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful for treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Draba rupestris var. legitima include reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and promoting overall well-being. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential uses and effects.
